name: What's the deadline to file a claim for chemical exposure from years ago in Utah—I'm just now finding out I have health issues?

mainEntity.name: What's the deadline to file a claim for chemical exposure from years ago in Utah—I'm just now finding out I have health issues?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Utah, workers generally have two years from the date they discover their illness is work-related to file a claim. Because toxic exposure symptoms often take years to appear, the 'discovery rule' may protect your right to file even if the exposure happened long ago.
acceptedAnswer.description:
In Utah, you generally have two years from the date you discovered the injury and its cause to file a claim. However, toxic exposure cases are unique because symptoms often appear years after the actual contact with chemicals. This "discovery rule" means the clock typically starts when a medical professional confirms your illness is work-related, rather than your last day on the job. Waiting too long can result in a permanent bar from receiving benefits.

Several factors influence this timeline:
- The date of your official diagnosis linking the condition to work.
- Whether you reported the exposure to your supervisor when it happened.
- The specific statute of repose in your state (Utah vs. Idaho differ slightly).

You should immediately document your symptoms and seek a medical evaluation. Telling your doctor exactly what substances you worked with is critical for establishing the timeline. A legal review of your employment records can help determine if you are still within the filing window.
